title: Latent Degradation Residual State
summary: Recent F1 tyre degradation research models lap time as fuel-adjusted latent tyre pace with pit stops as state resets.
rationale: Recent F1 tyre degradation research models lap time as fuel-adjusted latent tyre pace with pit stops as state resets. The current features use raw degradation and ratios, but not a fitted per-stint residual state capturing whether a driver is degrading faster than expected for race, compound, and lap.
implementation_hint: Within each Race-Driver-Stint sorted by LapNumber, fit simple expanding/rolling residual features: lap_time minus race-lap median, residual slope over last 3-5 laps, residual acceleration, cumulative positive residual, and residual z-score within Race-Compound. Shift rolling features by one lap where possible to preserve causality.
expected_effect: May separate drivers approaching a stop because their pace trend is worsening, not just because TyreLife is high.
risk: Sparse stints and safety-car/outlier laps can add noise; clipping or robust medians are needed.
